meta.title: CJK / Unicode Failure Corpus
meta.tagline: Real CJK, IME, and Unicode/text-handling bugs in open-source libraries. Repro, affected libs, and the fix.
meta.sourceNote: Every entry maps to a real pull request authored by greymoth-jp. Titles, repos, URLs, and statuses are taken verbatim from the GitHub API.

ime-composition.label: IME composition
ime-composition.blurb: When a Japanese/Chinese/Korean user types, they press Enter to confirm an IME conversion (pick a kanji candidate). That same Enter often fires the component's own keydown / submit / select handler, so the form sends or an item is selected with half-finished text. The guard is one line: skip the handler while the composition is active (event.isComposing, or keyCode 229; in React read event.nativeEvent.isComposing).

kana-romaji.label: Kana / romaji
kana-romaji.blurb: Transliteration tables that drop or reverse kana. Round-trip is the oracle: kana to romaji and back should be stable, and a sibling (hiragana vs katakana) usually already does it right.

width-normalization.label: Width / normalization
width-normalization.blurb: Full-width to half-width, long-vowel marks, and kana range boundaries. Off-by-one range tables and missing digraphs silently corrupt text.

numeral.label: Numerals
numeral.blurb: Kanji numerals, including the daiji (traditional) forms used in legal and financial documents.

locale-leftover.label: Locale data
locale-leftover.blurb: Missing or wrong ja-JP strings: untranslated placeholders, mistranslations that flip meaning, labels left in English.

codegen-escape.label: Codegen escape
codegen-escape.blurb: Generators that interpolate text without escaping it. Special characters (including CJK and non-ASCII identifiers) leak into the output and break it.

regex-roundtrip.label: Regex roundtrip
regex-roundtrip.blurb: Parse to AST and back. A character that is special in one position (a leading caret, a hyphen) can be emitted unescaped and change what the pattern matches.

entries.title: n-dynamic-tags commits a tag on the Enter that confirms an IME conversion
entries.symptom: Pressing Enter to confirm a kana to kanji conversion in an n-dynamic-tags input creates a tag from the in-progress text instead of just finishing the conversion.
entries.repro:
1. Render <n-dynamic-tags> and focus its input.
2. Switch to a Japanese IME, type "とうきょう", press Space to convert to 東京.
3. Press Enter to pick the candidate.
4. A tag is added from the unconfirmed text before the conversion commits.
entries.fix: Skip tag creation while e.isComposing is true; only act on the Enter that fires after compositionend.

entries.title: Chat box sends the message on the Enter that confirms an IME candidate
entries.symptom: Confirming a Japanese IME candidate with Enter in the chat box sends the message instead of committing the conversion.
entries.repro:
1. Open the chat input.
2. With a CJK IME, type a phrase and press Space to get conversion candidates.
3. Press Enter to choose a candidate.
4. The message sends with the unconfirmed text.
entries.fix: Return early when e.nativeEvent.isComposing is true. React's synthetic KeyboardEvent has no isComposing field, so read it off nativeEvent (the DOM event), not e.isComposing.

entries.title: Command palette runs the highlighted command on the IME confirm Enter
entries.symptom: Typing a CJK query in the command palette and pressing Enter to confirm the IME runs the highlighted command instead of finishing the conversion.
entries.repro:
1. Open the command palette.
2. Compose a Japanese search term with the IME.
3. Press Enter to commit the conversion.
4. The first/highlighted command fires prematurely.
entries.fix: Add `if (e.isComposing) return` at the top of the palette input keydown handler. Svelte exposes the native KeyboardEvent, so e.isComposing works directly.

entries.title: Confirming a CJK @-mention with Enter replaces it with the highlighted option (Safari)
entries.symptom: On Safari/WebKit, composing an @-mention name in CJK and pressing Enter to confirm the IME replaces the text with whatever mention is highlighted.
entries.repro:
1. In Safari, type @ then compose a Japanese name with the IME.
2. Press Enter to confirm the kanji conversion.
3. The composing text is replaced by the highlighted mention option.
entries.fix: In the ENTER branch, return early on event.nativeEvent.isComposing before preventDefault. WebKit reports the commit keydown as which===Enter + isComposing:true; Chromium reports keyCode 229 and never enters this branch.

entries.title: Enter that confirms an IME composition also selects the active option
entries.symptom: The Enter used to commit an IME composition also selects the currently highlighted option in a searchable Select.
entries.repro:
1. Open a searchable <Select>.
2. Type a CJK query with the IME and press Enter to confirm the conversion.
3. The active option is selected with the unfinished query.
entries.fix: Track composition state (compositionstart/compositionend) and skip option selection while composing.

entries.title: time-picker handles keys during IME composition
entries.symptom: The time-picker's key handler fires while an IME composition is active, so keystrokes meant for the IME mutate the time value.
entries.repro:
1. Focus the time-picker input.
2. Begin an IME composition.
3. Composition keystrokes are intercepted by the picker's key handler instead of the IME.
entries.fix: Skip the picker's keydown handling while the composition is active (isComposing guard).

entries.title: VAutocomplete keydown listener fires during IME composition
entries.symptom: VAutocomplete's keydown listener triggers during IME composition, so confirming a CJK query navigates or selects options.
entries.repro:
1. Open a VAutocomplete.
2. Compose a Japanese query, press Enter/arrow to confirm.
3. The keydown listener acts on the composing keys.
entries.fix: Bail out of the keydown listener when e.isComposing is true.

entries.title: defineShortcuts fires global shortcuts while typing with an IME
entries.symptom: Single-key shortcuts registered with defineShortcuts fire while composing text, so romaji keystrokes trigger app shortcuts mid-composition.
entries.repro:
1. Register a single-key shortcut (e.g. 'g').
2. In an input, compose Japanese text whose romaji includes that key.
3. The shortcut fires during composition.
entries.fix: Ignore key events when e.isComposing (or keyCode 229) before dispatching shortcuts.

entries.title: color-picker channel input handles Enter during IME composition
entries.symptom: A color-picker channel input runs its Enter handler while an IME composition is active, committing before the composition ends.
entries.repro:
1. Focus a color channel input.
2. Trigger an IME composition and press Enter.
3. The channel commit fires before compositionend.
entries.fix: Guard the channel input's Enter handler with isComposing.

entries.title: Inline file rename submits while the IME is composing
entries.symptom: Renaming a file inline and pressing Enter to confirm a CJK name submits the rename mid-composition.
entries.repro:
1. Start an inline file rename.
2. Compose a Japanese filename with the IME.
3. Press Enter to confirm the conversion; the rename submits with the unfinished name.
entries.fix: Skip the rename submit while the IME is composing (isComposing guard).

entries.title: Angular chat input submits on the IME confirm Enter
entries.symptom: The Angular chat input submits the message on the Enter that confirms an IME composition.
entries.repro:
1. Type a CJK message in the Angular chat input.
2. Press Enter to confirm the IME conversion; the message sends early.
entries.fix: Check event.isComposing (Angular passes the native KeyboardEvent) before submitting.

entries.title: Composer sends on the Enter that confirms an IME composition
entries.symptom: The Composer sends the message on the Enter used to confirm an IME composition.
entries.repro:
1. Compose CJK text in the Composer.
2. Press Enter to pick a candidate; the message sends instead of committing.
entries.fix: Skip send when e.nativeEvent.isComposing is true.

entries.title: Katakana ン loses the syllabic-n apostrophe before vowels and Y
entries.symptom: Katakana ン before a vowel or Y is romanized without the apostrophe, unlike hiragana ん. シンヨウ becomes SHINYOU (should be SHIN'YOU), so it collides with シニョウ.
entries.fix: Map katakana ン to N' (matching hiragana) and add the ンー long-vowel digraph as N', so N is never a katakana map key and toKatakana still round-trips (PAN to パン).

entries.title: Missing っでぃ (ddi) sokuon entry for Hepburn and Kunrei
entries.symptom: The geminated d + small i sequence っでぃ (ddi) has no Hepburn or Kunrei entry, so loanword spellings that use it romanize incorrectly.
entries.repro: Convert a word containing っでぃ; the doubled d before the small i is not produced.
entries.fix: Add the missing っでぃ (ddi) sokuon entry to both the Hepburn and Kunrei tables.

entries.title: Historical kana ゐ/ゑ missing from the hiragana/katakana mapping
entries.symptom: The historical kana ゐ (wi) and ゑ (we) are absent from the mapping, so text containing them is dropped or left unconverted.
entries.repro: Convert text containing ゐ or ゑ (or katakana ヰ/ヱ); the characters pass through unmapped.
entries.fix: Add ゐ/ゑ and their katakana counterparts to the kana mapping.

entries.title: kana2alphabet does not romanize small katakana ヵ/ヶ
entries.symptom: kana2alphabet does not handle the small katakana ヵ/ヶ (small ka/ke), so counters like 一ヶ月 are mis-romanized.
entries.repro:
import jaconv
jaconv.kana2alphabet('ヶ')  # small ke not handled
entries.fix: Add the small ka/ke mappings to kana2alphabet.

entries.title: Reversed ヲ/ヺ voiced-mark mapping in addVoicedMarks and combineSoundMarks
entries.symptom: The ヲ to ヺ voiced-mark mapping is reversed, so adding or stripping a dakuten on ヲ produces the wrong character.
entries.repro: Add a voiced mark to ヲ; expected ヺ, got the wrong glyph because the mapping is reversed.
entries.fix: Correct the reversed ヲ/ヺ entry in both addVoicedMarks and combineSoundMarks.

entries.title: ー (long-vowel mark) expands incorrectly after katakana ヒ and ビ
entries.symptom: During normalization, the chōonpu (ー) is expanded with the wrong vowel after katakana ヒ and ビ.
entries.repro: Normalize ヒー / ビー; the long-vowel expansion produces the wrong vowel for these two kana.
entries.fix: Fix the ー-expansion table entries for ヒ and ビ.

entries.title: Width/kana conversion drops the first and last character of each range
entries.symptom: Full-width/half-width and kana range conversions skip the boundary characters of each range (！ ～ ぁ ゖ ...), so edge code points like ！ (U+FF01) are not converted.
entries.repro: moji('！～').convert('ZE', 'HE').toString()  // boundary chars at range edges are skipped
entries.fix: Convert the first and last character of each range, not just the interior.

entries.title: kanji2number cannot parse 萬 (daiji form of 万)
entries.symptom: kanji2number cannot parse 萬, the daiji (大字) traditional form of 万 (10,000), so legal and financial documents that use 大字 numerals fail to convert.
entries.repro:
from kanjize import kanji2number
kanji2number('萬')  # not recognized as 10000
entries.fix: Map 萬 to 万 (10,000) in kanji2number.

entries.title: Wrong ja-JP Typography expand/collapse labels
entries.symptom: The ja-JP labels for Typography's expand/collapse control were incorrect, so Japanese users saw the wrong 展開/折りたたみ text.
entries.repro:
1. Set ConfigProvider locale to ja_JP.
2. Render <Typography.Paragraph ellipsis={{ expandable: true }}>.
3. The expand/collapse label is wrong.
entries.fix: Correct the ja-JP expand/collapse label strings.

entries.title: weekPlaceholder left untranslated in the Japanese locale
entries.symptom: weekPlaceholder was missing from the Japanese locale, so the week date-picker showed an English placeholder.
entries.repro: Use the date picker in week mode with the ja locale; the placeholder renders in English.
entries.fix: Add the Japanese weekPlaceholder translation.

entries.title: Japanese future times used 以内 (within) instead of 後 (later)
entries.symptom: Future timestamps in the ja locale used 以内 (within) instead of 後 (later), so '3 minutes from now' rendered as 3分以内 (within 3 minutes), which means the opposite.
entries.repro: timeago a future timestamp with the ja locale; it renders '3分以内' instead of '3分後'.
entries.fix: Use 後 for future time strings in the ja locale.

entries.title: Japanese day-of-month step description not scoped to a month
entries.symptom: The Japanese description for a day-of-month step was not scoped to a month, producing a mistranslated cron description.
entries.repro: cronstrue.toString('0 0 1-31/3 * *', { locale: 'ja' }); the description is not scoped to the month.
entries.fix: Scope the ja day-of-month step description to a month.

entries.title: Generator emits a leading ^ unescaped, inverting [a^] into [^a]
entries.symptom: Optimizing or regenerating a character class can move a literal ^ to the front and emit it unescaped, flipping the meaning: [a^] round-trips to [^a] (a negated class).
entries.repro:
const rt = require('regexp-tree')
rt.optimize('/[a^]/').toString() // '/[^a]/'  <- now matches the negation
entries.fix: In the generator, escape a leading literal ^ in a non-negative character class so generate(parse(x)) preserves meaning.

entries.title: Enum member names with special characters produce invalid TypeScript
entries.symptom: Enum member names containing special characters (including non-ASCII / CJK) are emitted unescaped, so the generated enum does not compile.
entries.repro: Generate types from a schema whose enum values contain quotes or special characters; the emitted enum has invalid member identifiers and tsc errors.
entries.fix: Escape enum member names so special characters produce valid output.

entries.title: Formatter over-escapes a mid-line # (C# becomes C\#)
entries.symptom: The formatter over-escapes a # in the middle of a line because the heading branch of the escape regex is not anchored to line start: 'C# is a language' becomes 'C\# is a language'.
entries.repro: format(parse('C# is a language')) // 'C\# is a language' - the '#' is escaped mid-sentence
entries.fix: Anchor the heading alternative: change #+\s to ^#+\s, mirroring the already-anchored ^* and ^> cases.
